Title: Research snapshot
Description: Collect the initial spine papers and lock the closest baselines
Acceptance: Snapshot identifies scope; nearest prior work; and evidence needs
Notes: Duplicate/split/insert rows as needed; re-validate after edits

Title: Baseline shortlist & competitive landscape
Description: Systematically identify 10-20 candidate baselines across 3 categories (direct_competitor / foundational / ablation_anchor). Record each in notes/design/baselines.csv with paper title; code URL; datasets; strengths; weaknesses. Select 4-8 for final comparison.
Acceptance: baselines.csv has >=10 candidates with >=4 selected; covers all 3 categories
Notes: See references/experiment-design.md Section 1

Title: Method positioning notes
Description: Write bullet-only positioning: what we change vs prior work and what we do not claim
Acceptance: Positioning notes contain at least 3 reviewer objections with responses

Title: Pipeline component inventory
Description: Design the method pipeline: identify all components; define input/output interfaces; mark novel vs standard components; specify ablation replacements. Record in notes/design/method-components.csv.
Acceptance: method-components.csv has >=3 components with is_novel and replaceable_by filled
Notes: See references/experiment-design.md Section 3

Title: Baseline identification & selection
Description: Verify selected baselines from R2: confirm code availability; reproduce or collect reported results on target datasets; flag any baselines that cannot be fairly compared. Update baselines.csv selected column.
Acceptance: All selected baselines have confirmed evaluation path (public code or paper-reported results)
Notes: Evaluation path confirmed for all selected baselines; case-specific reproduction awaits dataset finalization

Title: Experiment matrix (main comparison)
Description: Design the main comparison matrix: baselines x datasets x metrics. Ensure fair comparison (same splits; same preprocessing; same evaluation protocol). Record full matrix in notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=main_comparison.
Acceptance: experiment-matrix.csv has >=1 main_comparison row per selected baseline per dataset
Notes: Matrix recorded in notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv (dataset placeholders until case study fixed)

Title: Ablation matrix
Description: Design ablation experiments from method-components.csv: for each high-priority component define removal/degradation strategy and expected impact. Record in experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=ablation. Minimum 4 ablation factors.
Acceptance: experiment-matrix.csv has >=4 ablation rows; each links to a component_id
Notes: Recorded in notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv with component_id links

Title: Robustness/error analysis plan
Description: Define robustness checks (noise; distribution shift; domain transfer) and error analysis (failure case categories; sampling protocol). Record in experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=robustness.
Acceptance: experiment-matrix.csv has >=2 robustness rows with corruption severities defined
Notes: Recorded in notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv with explicit severity_levels

Title: Efficiency/scalability analysis
Description: Compare computational cost: parameter count (M); FLOPs (G); inference latency (ms) against selected baselines. Record in experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=efficiency.
Acceptance: experiment-matrix.csv has >=1 efficiency row with params/FLOPs/latency columns filled
Notes: Recorded in notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv (metrics planned; values pending experiments)

Title: Write experiment code
Description: Write training and evaluation scripts based on method-components.csv and experiment-matrix.csv. Include: model implementation; training loop; evaluation metrics computation; data loading. Code must be runnable with the configured runtime.
Acceptance: Training script and evaluation script exist; pass syntax check (python -m py_compile); use experiment-matrix metrics
Notes: Code structure should follow method-components.csv pipeline

Title: Write experiment runner and configs
Description: Create run_all.py (orchestrates all experiment types from experiment-matrix.csv) and YAML config files under experiments/configs/. Runner must accept --config flag and export results to paper/results/.
Acceptance: run_all.py exists; configs/ has at least one .yaml; run_all.py --help exits 0
Notes: Must read runtime config from paper.config.yaml

Title: Write experiments README
Description: Create experiments/README.md with: environment setup; dependency installation; how to run each experiment type; config file explanation; expected output paths; how to invoke results-backfill after completion.
Acceptance: README.md exists in experiments/; covers setup + run + output sections
Notes: Reference paper.config.yaml runtime section

Title: Run main comparison experiments
Description: Execute main comparison experiments per experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=main_comparison. Export results to paper/results/.
Acceptance: main_results.csv exists and matches experiment-matrix main_comparison rows
Notes: Runnable code or manual results import required

Title: Run ablation experiments
Description: Execute ablation experiments per experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=ablation. Export results.
Acceptance: Ablation results CSV exists; covers all high-priority ablation rows in experiment-matrix
Notes: Skip if user cannot run; mark Result_Status=planned

Title: Run robustness experiments
Description: Execute robustness experiments per experiment-matrix.csv rows with type=robustness. Export results.
Acceptance: Robustness results exist; cover all robustness rows in experiment-matrix
Notes: Skip if user cannot run; mark Result_Status=planned

Title: Export verified results to LaTeX
Description: Convert all verified experiment results to LaTeX input tables/figures under paper/results/
Acceptance: All verified results have corresponding .tex input files; no -- placeholders in tables

Title: Introduction
Description: Draft the introduction and contribution framing
Acceptance: Section drafted with citations and bounded claims
Notes: Keep claim language aligned with evidence matrix

Title: Related work
Description: Draft a minimal (non-survey) related work that distinguishes this paper
Acceptance: Section distinguishes at least 3 closest lines of work with citations

Title: Method
Description: Draft the method section based on notes/design/method-components.csv: describe each component; justify design choices; include pipeline diagram
Acceptance: Method includes clear inputs/outputs and design rationale for novel components
Notes: Reference method-components.csv for component details

Title: Experimental setup
Description: Draft experimental setup based on notes/design/experiment-matrix.csv and baselines.csv: datasets; baselines; metrics; implementation details; reproducibility info
Acceptance: Setup includes reproducibility details and exact protocol

Title: Results (placeholder-safe)
Description: Draft results section with explicit hypothesis-safe placeholder language. Do not include numbers or significance claims unless verified.
Acceptance: All result statements are hypothesis-safe; all tables/figures use -- or placeholders until verified
Notes: Do not invent numbers

Title: Limitations & ethics
Description: Draft limitations focusing on method constraints; failure modes; evidence gaps
Acceptance: Limitations include at least 3 concrete limitations tied to evidence status

Title: Conclusion
Description: Draft conclusion using hypothesis-safe language when results are not yet verified; upgrade to factual claims only after backfill.
Acceptance: Conclusion contains no fabricated outcomes; uses (hypothesis)/[Pending] tags until verified

Title: Abstract
Description: Draft abstract: problem; method; setting; key outcome only if verified; implication. Must be self-contained. See references/abstract-conclusion-guide.md.
Acceptance: Abstract is substantive (not a stub); contains specific verified numbers if available; <=250 words
Notes: Write only after Results and Conclusion are DONE

Title: System overview figure
Description: Generate TikZ system overview showing problem setting and where the method fits. Derive from method-components.csv and domain context. See references/figure-generation-guide.md.
Acceptance: TikZ figure compiles; is referenced in Introduction; shows problem + method modules
Notes: Pattern: system-overview from figure-generation-guide.md

Title: Method architecture diagram
Description: Generate TikZ method architecture diagram from method-components.csv. Novel components highlighted. See references/figure-generation-guide.md.
Acceptance: TikZ figure compiles; shows all pipeline components; novel ones visually distinguished
Notes: Pattern: method-architecture from figure-generation-guide.md

Title: Case-study inflow time series
Description: Generate a publication-quality figure showing daily inflow time series for the three reservoirs with train/tune/test split boundaries.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Experimental Setup; shows chronological splits clearly
Notes: Generated via experiments/render_figures.py from ResOpsUS2 time series

Title: Forecast skill per reservoir
Description: Generate a figure that breaks down CRPS/RMSE by reservoir for key forecast baselines and the rolling AR(p) forecaster.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Results; supports bounded per-reservoir discussion
Notes: Generated from paper/results/forecast_results_per_dam.json

Title: Operational value per reservoir
Description: Generate a figure showing objective change (relative to baseline) and violation rate per reservoir across operational pipelines.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Results; supports heterogeneity discussion and violation concentration claim
Notes: Generated from paper/results/main_results_per_dam.json

Title: Robustness plot: inflow scaling
Description: Generate a plot of objective change (%) and violation-rate change (pp) under multiplicative inflow scaling (relative to baseline) for key methods.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Robustness; axes and sign conventions match caption; derived from robustness_results.csv
Notes: Generated from paper/results/robustness_results.csv

Title: Robustness heatmap: sparse/delayed obs
Description: Generate a heatmap figure summarizing objective change and violation rate under sparse/delayed observations for rolling calibration.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Robustness; clearly shows sensitivity surface
Notes: Generated from paper/results/robustness_results.csv

Title: Runtime breakdown figure
Description: Generate a runtime breakdown figure separating rolling-calibration time and dispatch time per cycle.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Efficiency; consistent with efficiency table
Notes: Generated from paper/results/efficiency_results.csv

Title: Ablation summary figure
Description: Generate a compact ablation summary figure reporting objective change (%) and violation-rate change (pp) relative to the full method.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Ablation Studies; labels do not overlap; derived from ablation_results.csv
Notes: Generated from paper/results/ablation_results.csv via experiments/render_figures.py

Title: Forecast calibration tradeoff
Description: Generate a figure showing CRPS versus nominal 80% coverage (calibration vs sharpness) for key forecasters broken down by reservoir.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Forecast Skill; supports bounded calibration discussion
Notes: Generated from paper/results/forecast_results_per_dam.json via experiments/render_figures.py

Title: Compute--value tradeoff
Description: Generate a compute--value tradeoff figure plotting runtime per cycle (ms) versus objective change (%) relative to the static baseline aggregated across reservoirs.
Acceptance: Figure compiles; is referenced in Efficiency; derived from main_results_per_dam.json and main_results_timing_per_dam.json
Notes: Generated via experiments/render_figures.py; uses per-dam runtime and objective deltas

Title: Method details (equations)
Description: Expand the Method section with an explicit formulation of rolling calibration (RLS) probabilistic scenario generation MPC objective/constraints and feedback inflation.
Acceptance: Method is more detailed and reproducible; no fabricated claims
Notes: Added equations aligned with implementation in experiments/sim/rollout.py

Title: Results expanded with per-reservoir plots
Description: Expand Results narrative to discuss heterogeneity across reservoirs and tie bounded claims to the new figures.
Acceptance: Results discuss per-reservoir breakdown and robustness with figure support; no new unverified claims
Notes: Added figure references and heterogeneous interpretation

Title: Discussion
Description: Add a Discussion section interpreting forecast-skill vs operational-value results; role of uncertainty propagation and feedback; and feasibility implications.
Acceptance: Discussion is grounded in verified results/figures and existing citations; no fabricated claims
Notes: Added Discussion section with bounded statements and figure references

Title: Integrate calibration/runtime tradeoff figures
Description: Integrate the calibration tradeoff and compute--value tradeoff figures into the Results narrative with bounded interpretation aligned to the reported summary metrics.
Acceptance: New figures are introduced and interpreted without changing reported numbers; no new unverified claims
Notes: Added references to Fig. forecast_calibration_tradeoff and Fig. runtime_value_tradeoff

Title: Rhythm refinement
Description: Apply latex-rhythm-refiner after all writing and visual issues are DONE
Acceptance: All sections refined and citations preserved
Notes: Applied latex-rhythm-refiner pass to paper/main.tex after adding expanded method/results and new figures

Title: Citation verification
Description: Verify citations; source policy; compilation status
Acceptance: All citation audits pass and BibTeX lint is clean
Notes: Passed: compile_paper.py --check-warnings --fail-on-warnings; citation_policy lint-bib audit-bib verify-bib audit-tex
